title: narcissus coronatus subsp. lusitanicus
intro: Narcissus coronatus subsp. lusitanicus, also known as the Portuguese daffodil or white daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and as a purgative. The health benefits of Narcissus coronatus subsp. lusitanicus include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in traditional medicine, typically in the form of infusions or decoctions, and are said to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
